source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.4.1–1.4.12 — presentation, assessment and referral

source.evidence_paraphrase: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

assessment.text: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.
assessment.rationale: Directly supports mapped ultrasound review and cautions against relying on LMP or a single early scan alone.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines clinician-performed transvaginal or selected transabdominal ultrasound and cautions against overinterpreting one early scan or LMP alone.

assessment.text: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.
assessment.rationale: Directly supports mapped hCG review, worsening-symptom warnings, and clinician-arranged follow-up without autonomous interpretation.

source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

rewrites.final_wording: Lists pain, amenorrhoea, bleeding, gastrointestinal symptoms, dizziness or syncope, shoulder-tip pain, urinary symptoms, tenderness, haemodynamic signs, pregnancy testing, and escalation criteria.

source.title: Ectopic pregnancy and miscarriage: diagnosis and initial management
source.population: Women, trans men, and non-binary people with pain, bleeding, or other complications in early pregnancy up to 13 completed weeks.
source.setting: Primary, emergency, gynaecology, and early pregnancy assessment services.
source.exclusions: Exact for early-pregnancy pain, bleeding, ectopic-pregnancy warning signs, ultrasound, and hCG assessment; partial for uncomplicated pregnancy symptoms and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Overview and recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.4 — early pregnancy assessment services

source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines the up-to-13-week complication population and services offering specialist assessment, ultrasound, serum hCG review, and timely referral.

rewrites.final_wording: Requires symptoms to take priority over hCG, limits hCG interpretation to appropriate clinical use, and requires clear worsening-symptom access and follow-up.
